clinical_coherence.notes: Clinically coherent FND presentation in 34F with anxiety, depression, IBS, and childhood sexual abuse history. Positive Hoover sign, give-way weakness, midline splitting, PNES witnessed. Social history mentions 'restaurant manager' as occupation but HPI says 'elementary school teacher' - minor inconsistency in occupational details. Otherwise a plausible and well-constructed clinical picture.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG captured one event with no ictal correlate. MRI normal. Labs normal with post-event prolactin normal. All outputs consistent with FND and concordant with ground truth.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD F44.4 correct. Differential comprehensive. Critical actions and contraindicated actions clinically sound. Emphasis on positive diagnostic approach and anti-seizure medication avoidance.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate. Clear positive functional signs, classic demographics, and textbook PNES presentation.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 34F African American with anxiety, IBS, and childhood trauma is realistic. Occupation discrepancy between social history (restaurant manager) and HPI (elementary school teacher) is a data integrity issue but does not affect clinical plausibility.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case reinforcing positive FND diagnostic approach. Detailed PNES semiology description, comprehensive psychiatric evaluation, and treatment planning with CBT and physiotherapy.

issues_found: Occupation inconsistency: social_history says 'restaurant manager' but HPI says 'elementary school teacher'
strengths: Well-documented positive functional signs in both exam and observed event
Detailed psychiatric evaluation with DSM-5 diagnoses
Literature search output with relevant treatment evidence
